judgment.summary: Okta features strong cash generation and high gross margins but is weighed down by high leverage and slowing revenue growth.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last reported EPS of 0.90 beat the 0.85 estimate (6.3% surprise); next earnings date is 2026-05-28.
positives: PEG ratio 0.85 — suggests the stock is undervalued relative to its growth.
Gross margin 77.4% — indicates strong core product profitability.
Total cash $2.55B vs total debt $451M — shows a very healthy net cash position.
Free cash flow (TTM) $836.12M — reflects strong ability to generate cash.
negatives: Debt/Equity 6.44 — indicates a high level of leverage on the balance sheet.
Revenue growth 11.6% — shows relatively slow top-line expansion.
Analyst growth est for current Q -0.8% — suggests a potential near-term contraction in growth.

news.paragraph: Okta is facing mixed sentiment as Argus Research raised its price target to $75.00, even as the stock has recently experienced downward pressure. While broader industry news highlights the growing role of AI in identity security, there are no immediate company-specific catalysts such as earnings or product launches reported in the recent headlines.
